cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure out this soccer position swapping problem. So, there are five people on the team: Alice, Bob, Claire, Dave, and Eve. At the start of the match, they have specific positions assigned. Let me list those first to keep track. Initial positions: - Alice: right winger - Bob: right midfielder - Claire: left winger - Dave: cheerleader - Eve: goalkeeper Then, there are several swaps that happen in a specific order. Let me go through each swap step by step and track who is in which position after each swap. The key here is to follow each swap carefully and not mix up the order. The first swap is between Dave and Bob. Let me write that down: First swap: Dave and Bob trade positions. So initially, Bob was right midfielder, and Dave was cheerleader. After swapping, Bob becomes cheerleader and Dave becomes right midfielder. So after first swap: - Alice: right winger - Bob: cheerleader - Claire: left winger - Dave: right midfielder - Eve: goalkeeper Okay, moving on to the second swap: Dave and Eve trade positions. Second swap: Dave (right midfielder) and Eve (goalkeeper) swap. So now Dave becomes goalkeeper and Eve becomes right midfielder. After second swap: - Alice: right winger - Bob: cheerleader - Claire: left winger - Dave: goalkeeper - Eve: right midfielder Third swap: Alice and Bob trade positions. Third swap: Alice (right winger) and Bob (cheerleader) swap. So Alice becomes cheerleader and Bob becomes right winger. After third swap: - Alice: cheerleader - Bob: right winger - Claire: left winger - Dave: goalkeeper - Eve: right midfielder Fourth swap: Dave and Claire trade positions. Fourth swap: Dave (goalkeeper) and Claire (left winger) swap. So Dave becomes left winger and Claire becomes goalkeeper.
